macro_events.event_summary: Kevin Warsh survives a contentious Senate confirmation hearing with his proposed Fed "regime-change" agenda (hawkish, anti-QE, shrink-the-balance-sheet, more rules-based policy) still intact, raising odds he becomes Fed Chair.
primary_drivers: Hawkish Fed leadership repricing: higher real rates / steeper curve / reduced balance sheet support
Reduced implicit 'Fed put' — less accommodation for risk assets in stress
Stronger USD expectations from tighter-for-longer framework

beneficiaries.rationale: Schwab earnings are highly sensitive to the short end and to bank reserves; a Warsh regime that keeps rates higher for longer and continues QT supports net interest margin on its cash sweep and reduces the deposit-flight concern that hurt it in 2023.

A more rules-based, credible anti-inflation Fed tends to compress credit spreads over time and supports issuance volumes — Moody's ratings revenue is directly leveraged to debt issuance activity under a stable, predictable policy regime.

Regime change at the Fed with more policy volatility and genuine two-way rate risk (vs. forward-guidance suppression) drives rates futures and options volume — CME's interest rate complex is its largest revenue segment.

losers.rationale: Extreme-multiple momentum name whose valuation is almost entirely terminal-value/discount-rate sensitive; a credible hawkish regime change compresses the multiple that drove the 2025 run.
MicroStrategy is a levered bitcoin proxy; bitcoin trades as a liquidity/debasement asset and underperforms when the Fed credibly commits to balance-sheet shrinkage and positive real rates.

headlines: Analysis: Warsh emerges from a difficult hearing with his Fed 'regime-change' plan intact
